comment: GFO formalizes symbol structure as a type-level category; that overlaps the candidate concept's symbol/sign vehicle scope, but the kind mismatch keeps this short of close-match.

All four terms are relation-types, the same kind of thing `information-artifact.md` lists `Symbolization`/`Signification` as considered and rejected for its own scope. `Name` and `NameEvent` are excluded from this mapping — see `uncertainty-006`.
No dedicated sign/signifier class found in core BFO.
No dedicated sign/signifier class found in core DOLCE-Lite.
The comprehensive UFO paper does not reify a symbol/sign class in the searched text.
TUpper's fetched files contain no dedicated sign/representation class.
